mission.title: Install and Configure Koordinator on Kubernetes
mission.description: Koordinator is a QoS-based scheduling system designed to optimize resource utilization and improve the performance of Kubernetes workloads such as microservices and big data jobs. Installing Koordinator allows you to enhance your Kubernetes cluster's efficiency and reliability.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Koordinator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add koordinator https://koordinator-sh.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Koordinator
steps.description:
Install Koordinator using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'koordinator' and deploy the Koordinator chart.
```bash
helm install koordinator koordinator/koordinator --namespace koordinator --create-namespace --version 1.7.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Koordinator pods are running successfully in the 'koordinator' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n koordinator
```

steps.title: Access Koordinator services
steps.description:
Port-forward to access Koordinator services locally for testing.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/koordinator -n koordinator 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Koordinator Helm release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall koordinator --namespace koordinator
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with Koordinator.
```bash
kubectl delete crd clustercolocationprofiles.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd devices.scheduling.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd podmigrationjobs.scheduling.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd reservations.scheduling.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd nodemetrics.slo.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd nodeslos.slo.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd elasticquotas.scheduling.sigs.k8s.io
kubectl delete crd podgroups.scheduling.sigs.k8s.io
kubectl delete crd noderesourcetopologies.topology.node.k8s.io
kubectl delete crd elasticquotaprofiles.quota.koordinator.sh
kubectl delete crd recommendations.analysis.koordinator.sh
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'koordinator' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace koordinator
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Backup the current configuration and state of Koordinator before upgrading.
```bash
kubectl get all -n koordinator -o yaml > koordinator-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Koordinator Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Koordinator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Koordinator installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade koordinator koordinator/koordinator --namespace koordinator --version 1.7.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Koordinator pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n koordinator
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If Koordinator pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for resource availability in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n koordinator
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the pod logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n koordinator
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, verify that the service is running and check the port-forwarding setup.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n koordinator
kubectl port-forward svc/koordinator -n koordinator 8080:80
```

troubleshooting.title: High resource usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high resource usage, check the resource limits set on the Koordinator deployment and adjust as necessary.
```bash
kubectl get deployment koordinator -n koordinator -o yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Koordinator will have all pods running in the 'koordinator' namespace, and you will be able to access the services via port-forwarding. The configuration should be optimized for your workloads.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add koordinator https://koordinator-sh.github.io/charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
